title: Files, Directories, Links, and Metadata · Changed-Host Lab
summary: Prepare a service workspace while preserving names, metadata, link intent, and the ability to reverse every operation. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a reversible filesystem-change plan and evidence tree with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify regular files, directories, symbolic links, devices, sockets, and pipes from metadata rather than filename appearance.
Use mkdir, touch, cp, mv, and rm with explicit targets, collision checks, and recovery-aware previews.
Predict inode, path, deletion, filesystem, and permission behavior for hard and symbolic links.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.
